message:
So last winter, my neighbor kept rolling his eyes whenever I'd mention checking our sump pump and clearing gutters before the heavy rains. Fast-forward to that first nasty storm, and I'm standing in his driveway while he's frantically bailing water from his basement. The look on his face when he asked if *I* was dealing with flooding too? I could've had my "I told you so" moment, but honestly, I just felt relieved. Turns out a little quiet prep means no drama—not because you're paranoid, but because you can actually just... live. No stress, no basement pools, no midnight panic calls.

Have you found that doing a few simple checks actually just lets you sleep better when storms roll in?
